ref: They answered, “Let's ask Rebekah what she wants to do.”
text: Le Rebeka aana ni uninja bui ke, “Cha ti yin Rebeka ki nan baa u lik.”
translated: Rebeka said to the man, "I am Rebeka, who are you?"

ref: When God heard the boy crying, the angel of God called out to Hagar from heaven and said, “Hagar, why are you worried? Don't be afraid. I have heard your son crying.
text: Uwumbɔr nan ŋun ubo ngbaan aah wii pu na, le upii ngbaan nan ŋun Uwumbɔr aaneel nyan ni paacham ki baa u ke, “Hagar, ba muk si? Taa san ijawaan, Uwumbɔr ŋun saabo aah wii pu na.
translated: Then the angel of the Lord appeared to her and said, "Hagar, where are you going? Return to your mistress, Sarai, for I will greatly increase her descendants and she will have many nations descended from her."

ref: You will live there as a foreigner, but I will be with you and bless you. I will keep my promise to your father Abraham by giving this land to you and your descendants.
text: Li koo do, le m ga li bi aa chee, ki ŋa tinyoor ŋa aa pu. M ban ke m di kitiŋ kee mɔmɔk tii sin ni aayaabitiib la. Maah nan puu tipuur ti tii aate Abraham na, m ga li joo ti.
translated: This is the way, and I will show you the way, and I will guide you. I will teach you the way of the Lord, so that you may walk in his ways.  I will show you the way of Abraham, and I will guide you in it.

ref: One day, Joseph went to Potiphar's house to do his work, and none of the other servants were there.
text: Daalbaadaal le Josef nan koo waaninkpel aadiik ni u ti tun lituln. N‑yoonn ngbaan binaagbiib biken mu aa nan bi lichiln ni.
translated: Josef, I have a problem for you. I want to go to the market but I don't have money.

ref: and said to Abram: “I bless you in the name of God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
text: le ki ŋa tinyoor ŋa u pu, ki bui ke, “Uwumbɔr u kaa kpa ŋeen aatɔ, ki naan paacham ni kitiŋ na, ŋa tinyoor ŋa Abram pu.
translated: This is the story of how Abram became a great man. He was a man of great faith, and he trusted in God. He followed God's commands, and God blessed him.

ref: God gave them his blessing and said: Have a lot of children! Fill the earth with people and bring it under your control. Rule over the fish in the ocean, the birds in the sky, and every animal on the earth.
text: le ki ŋa tinyoor ŋa bi pu, le ki bui ke, “Maa, ki pɔr, aan ki gbiin kitiŋ kee pu, ki li lik kitiŋ ngbaan. Li kpa mpɔɔn ijan, ni inyoon, ni tiwan nimɔk fu ki baar kitiŋ pu na.”
translated: The child said to his mother, "Mother, I am hungry. I want to eat something, I want to eat something good. I want to eat rice, beans, and meat. I want to eat something tasty."

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: Tɔ, m ga puu tipuur tii si. Aa ni aapuu, ni saabim, ni bipuutiib koo buŋɔb ngbaan ni.
translated: Yes, I am going to the market to buy things. I will go with my mother, my father, and my siblings.

ref: I'll have some water brought, so you can wash your feet, then you can rest under a tree.
text: Cha m ban ni nnyun nan tii nimi, aan ni finn nitaa man; kal busub bue taab, ki fuur man.
translated: If you do not want to go with me, I will go alone; I will not wait for you, I will go.

ref: and he told them: Your father isn't as friendly with me as he used to be, but the God my ancestors worshiped has been on my side.
text: Baah fuu ni na le u bui bi ke, “M bee ke nite aa ki gee maabɔr ke waah nan gee mi njan pu na; tɔ, nte Aawumbɔr bi m chee.
translated: He said to me, "I will not let you go. I will not let you go until you give me your blessing; otherwise, I will not let you go."

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: See ni mɔmɔk bui u ke, ‘Een, saanaagbiija Jakob le bi kɔnkɔni do ki choo na.’” Jakob nan dakl ke, “Nibaakan Esɔ yaa kan ipiin ngbaan kan, u ga li gee mi, aan ti yaa kan tɔb kan, u ga di cha pinn mi.”
translated: See ni mɔmɔk bui u ke, ‘Een, saanaagbiija Jakob le bi kɔnkɔni do ki choo na.’” Jakob nan dakl ke, “Nibaakan Esɔ yaa kan ipiin ngbaan kan, u ga li gee mi, aan ti yaa kan tɔb kan, u ga di cha pinn mi.”

ref: Later, when Hagar knew she was going to have a baby, she became proud and treated Sarai hatefully.
text: Abram nan doon Hagar chee, le u dek lipuul. Hagar aah kan ke u kpa lipuul na, le waa ki lik pak udindaan Sarai.
translated: Abraham sent Hagar away because she was no longer able to bear him a child. Hagar was distressed and went to dwell in the wilderness of Sarai.

ref: But Cain had to go far from the Lord and live in the Land of Wandering, which is east of Eden. More and More People
text: Le Kainn siir Uwumbɔr chee, ki buen kitiŋ kibaa ni, bi yin ki ke “Chuun Gonni,” ki bi Edenn aawipuul wɔb la. Kainn aamaal
translated: The Kainn went to Uwumbɔr and said, "This is Chuun Gonni," and they pointed to Edenn who was sitting there. Kainn left.

ref: That's why Esau made his home in the hill country of Seir.
text: Kina pu le Esɔ nan buen ti bi Edom, ŋigongon aatiŋ ni.
translated: The man went to the market to buy something, he did not find it.

ref: Then Sarai said to Abram, “It's all your fault! I gave you my slave woman, but she has been hateful to me ever since she found out she was pregnant. You have done me wrong, and you will have to answer to the Lord for this.”
text: Sarai nan bui uchal Abram ke, “Ni ye saataani pu le Hagar aa ki lik pak mi. Min le nan di u tii si, le waah bee ke u kpa lipuul na, waa ki pak mi. Uwumbɔr le ga lik ki bee m ni si u aabɔr ŋan na.”
translated: Then Sarai said to Abram, “See, the Lord has kept me childless. Go, sleep with my maidservant Hagar; perhaps I can build a family through her.  The child she bears will be mine as if it were my own.”

ref: Afterwards, Rebekah and the young women who were to travel with her prepared to leave. Then they got on camels and left with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: N‑yoonn ngbaan le Rebeka ni waanaagbiisapɔmbim mɔmɔk gor, le ki diŋ laakumii tiib pu, ki dii Abraham aanaagbiija ngbaan buen.
translated: And Rebecca said to him, "I will give you a son, and you will call his name Isaac, for the Lord has heard my voice."
